purpose: Grounded source corpus for the Served EXPLAINER. These passages explain extracted facts after the deterministic verdict has been calculated; they never determine the verdict.

source_timeline.event: The Supreme Court adopted the 2025 Civil Rules package and transmitted it to Congress.
source_timeline.scope_note: The package amended Civil Rules 16 and 26 and added Rule 16.1. It did not amend Rule 45.

source_timeline.event: The 2025 amendments to Civil Rules 16 and 26 and new Rule 16.1 took effect.
source_timeline.scope_note: Served uses the official Federal Rules of Civil Procedure amended through this date. The Rule 45 passages in this corpus remain the current text because Rule 45 was not part of the 2025 effective amendments.

source_timeline.event: Served re-verified the selected Rule 45 passages against the U.S. Courts current-rules publication.
source_timeline.scope_note: This is a verification date, not a claim that the corpus will remain current indefinitely.

source_timeline.event: Proposed Rule 45 amendments are projected to take effect no earlier than this date if they complete the Rules Enabling Act process.
source_timeline.scope_note: Served does not treat proposed amendments as current law. The corpus must be re-verified before adopting any future text.

coverage_and_boundary.notice: Read before extending. This seed is intentionally limited to the July demo scope.

federal: United States District Court for the Central District of California
United States District Court for the Eastern District of California
United States District Court for the Northern District of California
United States District Court for the Southern District of California
United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit
july_demo_scope.fake_authority_fixture: D3's fictional Federal Legal Processing Bureau

not_in_directory_behavior.rule: Absence from the limited directory is not evidence of fraud, just as a RECAP docket miss is not evidence of fraud. P0-SPEC section 2 indicator 4 remains annotation-only.

out_of_scope_for_july: State courts beyond a possible Los Angeles Superior Court stub

global_guardrails: Do not use this corpus to decide SCAM, VERIFIED, or CANNOT_CONFIRM.
Quote only the official_quote field verbatim. Never reconstruct missing statutory or rule text.
Do not tell a user that a subpoena is valid, invalid, enforceable, or unenforceable based only on these passages.
Do not calculate a deadline unless the service date and applicable rule are known.
Do not calculate a garnishment amount without current pay data, deduction data, debt type, and applicable state law.
State law, local court rules, court orders, and case-specific facts may change the result.
Use neutral language and recommend confirmation with the issuing court or a qualified attorney for high-stakes decisions.

passages.title: What a federal subpoena should identify
passages.source_name: U.S. Courts — Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, amended through December 1, 2025
passages.plain_language: A federal subpoena should identify the issuing court, the case title and number, the person receiving the command, what is required, and when and where compliance is requested.
use_when: The READER extracted a federal court name, case caption, case number, recipient, command, deadline, or compliance location.
The product needs to explain which identifying fields are normally expected on a federal subpoena.
passages.do_not_infer: A missing field is a reason to verify the document, not proof that it is fake or legally invalid.

passages.title: The 100-mile place-of-compliance rule
passages.official_quote: within 100 miles of where the person resides, is employed, or regularly transacts business in person
passages.plain_language: For many federal subpoena commands, the permitted place of compliance is tied to a 100-mile radius. The exact rule differs for testimony, document production, and premises inspection.
use_when: The document states an in-person appearance, production, or inspection location.
The READER extracted both the recipient's location and the requested place of compliance.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not declare the subpoena defective without checking the command type, the recipient's relationship to the case, and the complete text of Rule 45(c).

passages.title: Deadline for written objections to document production
passages.source_name: U.S. Courts — Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, amended through December 1, 2025
passages.official_quote: The objection must be served before the earlier of the time specified for compliance or 14 days after the subpoena is served.
passages.plain_language: A person commanded to produce materials or permit inspection may be able to serve a written objection. The federal rule uses the earlier of the compliance time or 14 days after service.
use_when: The document commands production of materials or inspection.
A reliable service date and compliance date were extracted.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not present a calculated last day unless service is confirmed. Do not imply that an objection automatically cancels the subpoena.

passages.title: When a court must quash or modify a subpoena
passages.source_name: U.S. Courts — Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, amended through December 1, 2025
passages.plain_language: On a timely motion, the court where compliance is required must quash or modify a subpoena that allows too little time, exceeds Rule 45's geographic limits, requires protected material without an applicable exception, or creates undue burden.
use_when: The CHECKER found a possible timing, geographic, privilege, confidentiality, or burden issue.
The result screen needs to explain why attorney review may be appropriate.
passages.do_not_infer: Only a court can rule on a motion to quash or modify. Served must not announce that the subpoena has been quashed, is invalid, or may safely be ignored.

passages.title: Ignoring a served subpoena can have consequences
passages.source_name: U.S. Courts — Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, amended through December 1, 2025
passages.plain_language: A court may hold a properly served person in contempt for failing, without adequate excuse, to obey a subpoena or a related court order.
use_when: The document appears connected to a real case and commands action.
The user is considering ignoring the document.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not say contempt is automatic. Do not use this passage to pressure payment or to discourage a timely objection, motion, or request for legal help.

passages.title: What 'disposable earnings' means
passages.source_name: U.S. House Office of the Law Revision Counsel — 15 U.S.C. § 1672
passages.official_quote: that part of the earnings of any individual remaining after the deduction from those earnings of any amounts required by law to be withheld
passages.plain_language: The federal calculation starts with earnings left after deductions that the law requires. Voluntary deductions are not automatically subtracted for this federal calculation.
use_when: A document discusses withholding money from wages.
The EXPLAINER needs to define the base used for a federal garnishment limit.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not calculate disposable earnings from gross pay alone or assume every payroll deduction is legally required.

passages.title: General federal limit for ordinary wage garnishment
passages.source_name: U.S. House Office of the Law Revision Counsel — 15 U.S.C. § 1673
passages.official_quote: 25 per centum of his disposable earnings for that week
passages.plain_language: For an ordinary garnishment, federal law generally caps withholding at the lesser of 25% of weekly disposable earnings or the amount above 30 times the applicable federal minimum hourly wage.
use_when: The document concerns an ordinary consumer-debt wage garnishment.
The debt is not identified as support, bankruptcy, or a state or federal tax debt.
passages.do_not_infer: This is not a universal 25% allowance. The lesser federal test, exceptions, pay period, and more protective state law must be checked.

passages.title: Some debts use different garnishment rules
passages.source_name: U.S. House Office of the Law Revision Counsel — 15 U.S.C. § 1673
passages.plain_language: The ordinary federal limit does not apply in the same way to support orders, certain bankruptcy orders, or state and federal tax debts. Support orders have separate percentage limits.
use_when: The READER identifies child support, alimony, bankruptcy, or tax debt.
The debt type is unclear and the interface must explain why a simple percentage cannot be provided.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not apply the ordinary-debt formula to an excluded category and do not select a support percentage without the facts required by the statute.

passages.title: Federal protection from firing for one garnished debt
passages.source_name: U.S. House Office of the Law Revision Counsel — 15 U.S.C. § 1674
passages.official_quote: No employer may discharge any employee by reason of the fact that his earnings have been subjected to garnishment for any one indebtedness.
passages.plain_language: Federal law generally protects an employee from being fired because earnings were garnished for one debt.
use_when: The user asks whether a single wage garnishment automatically permits termination.
A document threatens job loss solely because of one garnished debt.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not promise protection for multiple debts or unrelated employment actions. State law may provide additional protection.

passages.title: A state may protect more earnings
passages.source_name: U.S. Department of Labor — Fact Sheet #30
passages.source_locator: Exceptions to Limitations on Wage Garnishments
passages.official_quote: the law resulting in the lower amount of earnings being garnished must be observed
passages.plain_language: The federal rule is a ceiling, not always the final answer. If applicable state law permits less to be taken, the more protective limit controls.
use_when: The user's state is known.
The interface explains why Served cannot calculate a final amount from federal law alone.
passages.do_not_infer: Do not state a state limit unless it comes from a separately verified, current state-law corpus.
